The pitch in one line: VST is the cleanest liquid expression of "who powers the AI." Every gigawatt of new data center
content:
Vistra Corp $VST is the biggest position I carry, about ten percent of the book.

The pitch in one line: VST is the cleanest liquid expression of "who powers the AI." Every gigawatt of new data center load in ERCOT and PJM has to come from somewhere, and VST is the largest independent power producer across those two grids combined. They already have signed twenty-year power purchase agreements with Amazon and Meta tied to the pending Cogentrix acquisition, which adds 5,500 megawatts and is expected to close mid-to-late 2026.

The reason it's been a frustrating hold until very recently: the stock peaked at $220 in February and spent most of March drawing down twenty-five percent on a combination of natural gas at seventeen-month lows compressing merchant margins, a $4 billion debt issuance to fund Cogentrix, and regulatory overhang on PJM capacity pricing. None of those things broke the thesis, but together they took the stock from priced-for-perfection back to priced-for-skepticism.

What's changed in the last week: the $4 billion notes priced cleanly at 4.55 to 5.55 percent, removing the binary refinancing risk Morgan Stanley had been flagging. Jefferies argued in a fresh note that at current levels VST is trading at roughly an eleven percent FY28 free cash flow yield ex buybacks, and that the price is not embedding any future data center contract wins. Sixteen of nineteen analysts still rate it Strong Buy with a mean price target of $234.

What's coming into May:

a) April 27 to May 1, the hyperscaler earnings cluster. Microsoft, Meta, Amazon, Google. If aggregate AI capex is reaffirmed near the $630 billion bar the market has set, VST gets to participate in the AI re-rating without needing to do anything itself.

b) May 7, VST Q1 earnings before the open. Consensus is modeling more than two hundred percent year-over-year EPS growth. Options flow is skewed bullish into the print. The setup carries real binary risk in either direction, but the trailing seventy-two times P/E that scares people normalizes to roughly fourteen times on FY27 estimates if guidance reaffirms.

c) May 12, April CPI. A hot print pushes the ten year back toward 4.55 percent and pressures every duration-sensitive AI infrastructure name including this one. A cool print is the cleaner setup.

My probability-weighted twelve-month target is $184, about thirteen percent above current. That sits below the consensus $234 because I'm carrying a higher bear weight on a possible PJM capacity-cap proposal and on the Martin Lake arc-flash incident from April 21 that has not shown up in price yet. But the asymmetry is what keeps it the largest line: a $225 bull case if Cogentrix closes on schedule and a fresh hyperscaler PPA lands, against a $120 bear case if Q1 misses or PJM acts.

This is what the math says for me, not what it should say for anyone else.

やるべきことはこれ。

content:
Claudeへの指示の出し方、完全に間違えているかもしれない。

というより、そもそも毎回プロンプトを書く必要がない。

やるべきことはこれ。

1. フォルダを作る

PCの中に「Claude Cowork」というフォルダを作る。

この中に入っているファイルが、
毎回チャットで入力していたプロンプトの代わりになる。

つまり、
「前提情報をもう一度貼る」
「文脈を毎回説明する」
みたいな作業が不要になる。

2. ABOUT ME フォルダを作る

中には、

・about-me.md
・anti-ai-writing-style.md

を入れる。

自分が何者なのか。
どんな文章を書くのか。
逆に、絶対に使わない言い回しは何か。

特に大事なのは、
「自分が採用する表現」よりも
「自分が拒否する表現」を多めに書くこと。

Claudeは作業前にこれを読んで、
あなたらしくないAIっぽい文章を避けやすくなる。

3. PROJECTS フォルダを作る

案件ごとに1つのサブフォルダを作る。

中には、

・brief.md
・references/
・drafts/

を入れる。

そしてClaudeにはこう伝える。

「PROJECTS/[プロジェクト名] の中身をすべて読んでから始めて」

これで、毎回15個も前提メッセージを貼る必要がなくなる。
進行中の作業は、全部この中に置けばいい。

4. TEMPLATES フォルダを作る

過去にうまくいった文章や構成を、
再利用できる型として保存しておく。

Claudeにはこう伝える。

「TEMPLATES/[テンプレート名] を使って」

さらに、

「長さ、構成、トーンを合わせて。中身だけ差し替えて」

と伝える。

これだけで、毎回ゼロから指示しなくてよくなる。

5. CLAUDE OUTPUTS フォルダを作る

Claudeが書き出す場所はここだけにする。

プロジェクトごとにサブフォルダを作り、
PROJECTSフォルダと同じ構成にする。

これで成果物がどこにあるか一瞬でわかる。

「Untitled 47.docx」みたいな謎ファイルが
画面に散らばることもなくなる。

6. Global Instructionsを設定する

毎回伝えるルールは、最初に1回だけ設定する。

Settings → Cowork → Edit Global Instructions

ここに、

・フォルダの使い方
・ファイル名のルール
・作業時のルール
・出力先のルール

を書いておく。

Claudeに毎回同じことを説明する必要がなくなる。

7. プロンプトは1文でいい

新しいプロンプトはこれだけ。

「私は[TASK]をしたいです。[SUCCESS CRITERIA]を満たすために、まずAskUserQuestionを使って始めてください。」

つまり、最初から完璧な指示を出す必要はない。

Claudeに質問させればいい。
あなたがClaudeに長文で説明する時代ではなく、
Claudeがあなたに必要なことを聞く時代になる。

8. Obsidianを使う

無料のObsidianで、
Claude Coworkフォルダをナレッジベースとして開く。

.mdファイルがGoogleドキュメントのように扱えるようになり、
編集、検索、リンク付けが一気に楽になる。

自分の頭の中とClaudeの作業記憶を、
1つのきれいな場所にまとめられる。

9. 大事なのは整合性

フォルダが文脈を話してくれる。
プロンプトはたった1行でいい。

Claudeは作業前にあなたへ質問し、
足りない情報を確認してから動く。

これが、古いプロンプト時代を終わらせるやり方。

毎回プロンプトを頑張って書くのではなく、
Claudeが迷わない環境を先に作る。

これが本当のAI活用。

title:
🚨BREAKING: OpenAI published a paper proving that ChatGPT will always make things up.

Not sometimes. Not until the next update. Always. They proved it with math.

Even with perfect training data and u
content:
🚨BREAKING: OpenAI published a paper proving that ChatGPT will always make things up.

Not sometimes. Not until the next update. Always. They proved it with math.

Even with perfect training data and unlimited computing power, AI models will still confidently tell you things that are completely false. This isn't a bug they're working on. It's baked into how these systems work at a fundamental level.

And their own numbers are brutal. OpenAI's o1 reasoning model hallucinates 16% of the time. Their newer o3 model? 33%. Their newest o4-mini? 48%. Nearly half of what their most recent model tells you could be fabricated. The "smarter" models are actually getting worse at telling the truth.

Here's why it can't be fixed. Language models work by predicting the next word based on probability. When they hit something uncertain, they don't pause. They don't flag it. They guess. And they guess with complete confidence, because that's exactly what they were trained to do.

The researchers looked at the 10 biggest AI benchmarks used to measure how good these models are. 9 out of 10 give the same score for saying "I don't know" as for giving a completely wrong answer: zero points. The entire testing system literally punishes honesty and rewards guessing.

So the AI learned the optimal strategy: always guess. Never admit uncertainty. Sound confident even when you're making it up.

OpenAI's proposed fix? Have ChatGPT say "I don't know" when it's unsure. Their own math shows this would mean roughly 30% of your questions get no answer. Imagine asking ChatGPT something three times out of ten and getting "I'm not confident enough to respond." Users would leave overnight. So the fix exists, but it would kill the product.

This isn't just OpenAI's problem. DeepMind and Tsinghua University independently reached the same conclusion. Three of the world's top AI labs, working separately, all agree: this is permanent.

Every time ChatGPT gives you an answer, ask yourself: is this real, or is it just a confident guess?

title:
Anthropic is the only AI company running a deflationary bundle.

Two years ago Claude Pro was a chat box. Today it's a chat box plus Artifacts, Skills, Connectors, Memory, and now Design. Five product
content:
Anthropic is the only AI company running a deflationary bundle.

Two years ago Claude Pro was a chat box. Today it's a chat box plus Artifacts, Skills, Connectors, Memory, and now Design. Five products in one seat. Still $20.

Run the parallel math. Adobe Creative Cloud is $60/month. Figma Pro is $15. Canva Pro is $15. Notion Business is $20. Replit Core is $25. Cursor Pro is $20. A solo builder paying for the equivalent stack is at $135-180/month and climbing.

OpenAI just shipped GPT-5.5 at $5.00 per million input tokens. GPT-5 launched at $0.63 eight months ago. Same model family, 8x price increase. Anthropic's consumer price stayed flat while the product count went 5x.

This is the AWS playbook from 2010-2015. Ship more, charge same, gut the margin of every point solution in the category. EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda. Each release cratered a public company. Rackspace got taken private after its market cap collapsed. Oracle's hardware business never recovered. Bundling at a flat price is how a $200B market gets restructured.

The $6B that came out of design software market caps this week is the public market starting the re-rate. Adobe at 8x revenue stops penciling when the bundler ships your category as a free feature inside an unrelated subscription.

The play has runway. Anthropic has shipped a new product into Claude Pro every 3-4 months for two years. The next launch is already priced at zero. So is the one after.

Full breakdown: https://t.co/Up3TviXTce

author: Shruti_0810

title:
The fastest way to ship an AI feature is Claude Code. The fastest way to kill it is skipping the feedback loop that tells you it failed.

Right now every product team is racing to ship AI. Prototyping
content:
The fastest way to ship an AI feature is Claude Code. The fastest way to kill it is skipping the feedback loop that tells you it failed.

Right now every product team is racing to ship AI. Prototyping with Claude Code, building agents, vibe coding features in an afternoon. The build cycle compressed from months to hours. The feedback cycle didn't compress at all.

Retention curves take 30, 60, 90 days to show you a feature failed. By then the PR cycle is done, the team moved on, and the data is pointing at a problem nobody can diagnose. You know something broke. You don't know what, when, or for whom.

The gap between shipping speed and learning speed is the actual risk in every AI product right now. You can build 10x faster. You can't learn 10x faster unless you instrument the feedback.

In-app surveys are the fastest closed-loop feedback tool that exists. Event-triggered, they fire two seconds after someone uses the feature. "Was this helpful?" Yes/No. 12% response rate. If the answer is No, a conditional follow-up asks what went wrong. Specific, attributable, same-day signal.

Sprig rebuilt their entire product around this principle. Surveys fire after checkout, after cart abandonment, after hitting a paywall. The feedback went from "it's fine" to "the shipping calculator didn't show international rates." Diagnosable within hours, not quarters.

The teams shipping AI the fastest are also accumulating the most undiagnosed failure. The fix is a 2-question survey attached to the feature, triggered by the action, reviewed the same day. Takes 30 minutes to set up. Most teams skip it because they tried surveys once, ran them badly, and concluded the tool was broken.

ทีม ThaiGPT Co.,LTD. เปิดตัว thClaws

AI agent workspace เขียนด้วย Rust ทั้งก้อน รัน local บนเครื่องเราเองได้ เปิด source Apache-2.0 / MIT release ปัจจุบัน v0.3.5

โครงการเริ่มจากการ reverse-engineer Claude Code หลัง Anthropic เผลอ publish sourcemap ขึ้น npm เมื่อมีนาคม 2026 ใช้เวลา 3 สัปดาห์ก็ ship

◆ insight จากการ reverse-engineer
ทีมพบว่า 1.6% ของ Claude Code เป็น AI decision logic อีก 98.4% เป็น deterministic harness ทั้งหมด
สรุปในบทความเปิดตัวว่า โมเดลไม่ใช่ตัวสินค้า harness ต่างหากที่เป็น

◆ surface 3 แบบ engine เดียว
Desktop GUI 4 tab (Chat / Terminal / Files / Team)
CLI REPL ผ่าน thclaws --cli
One-shot ผ่าน thclaws -p "..." สำหรับ CI

◆ multi-provider
Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Alibaba DashScope, OpenRouter, Ollama, Agentic Press
auto-detect จากชื่อโมเดล สลับ provider กลาง session ได้

◆ open standards
MCP สำหรับ tool, AGENTS.md สำหรับ project, SKILL.md สำหรับ workflow
plugins bundle ทุกอย่างใน manifest เดียว install ครั้งเดียว version เดียว

◆ memory + knowledge base
memory แบ่ง user / feedback / project / reference เก็บเป็น markdown แก้มือได้
KMS ใช้ grep + read ไม่มี embeddings ให้ดูแล

◆ Agent Teams
agent หลายตัว coordinate ผ่าน shared mailbox + task queue
แต่ละตัวอยู่ใน git worktree ของตัวเอง lead รวม branch เมื่อทั้งคู่เสร็จ

◆ binary 15 MB ตัวเดียว
boot ใต้ 200 ms วิ่งบน 6 platform
ไม่ต้องลง Node ไม่ต้องลง Python ไม่มี telemetry phone home

◆ ที่มาของทีม
ใน blog post ที่ 2 ทีมเล่าว่าเปิด repo ได้ไม่ถึง 24 ชม. contributor ครึ่งหนึ่งคือทีม Jimmy Software จาก 20 ปีก่อน ทีมที่เคยทำ Turjah คว้า Best Software จาก Pocket PC Magazine ทั้ง US และ Europe ในยุค 2000

◆ download
binary 6 target พร้อม SHA-256 sidecar ให้ verify
source อยู่บน https://t.co/T9hJbgY3dD
